source_ref: sak.mandhai, 'Dual Candle Strategy' (Dual Candlestick / Inside Bar H4), ForexFactory thread 1005994, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1005994-dual-candle-strategy
concept: H4 inside-bar (second candle fully contained by the first) breaks out via stop order in the direction of the first candle, filtered by Bollinger-Band position and RSI momentum.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic inside-bar breakout with BB(20,2) + RSI(14>50/<50) filters, pure MQL5, H4 FX data on .DWX, has a stop-loss so FTMO/DXZ-compatible; SL/TP delegated to an unseen Google-Sheet calculator (one worked example only), so exits need reconstruction.
notes: Entry FULL: long = bullish first candle + contained second candle both between upper & middle BB + RSI>50 -> buy-stop at first-candle high; short = mirror below middle BB + RSI<50 -> sell-stop at first-candle low; RSI read off the first candle. Exit VAGUE: SL/TP from a Google spreadsheet not posted; sole example EURUSD sell-stop 1.12202, SL 1.13402 (~120p), TP1/TP2/TP3 1.12002/1.11802/1.10702 (~20/40/150p) implies wide SL + laddered TPs. Author is a signal-seller (forexfiverr/tradingview signal service) -> reputability caveat. Inside-bar breakout is a heavily-mined generic pattern, not an uncorrelated driver. Thread otherwise chatter: a 'miracle indicator' tangent (no rules), a shared FXSniper Insidebar.ex4 helper indicator (not a distinct strategy), no backtest evidence beyond signal screenshots. No year stated in the thread.

source_ref: zeiman (Mantas, UK), "Trading System Simplicity", ForexFactory thread 1010582 (12 posts, n.d.), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1010582
concept: In a 100-EMA-defined trend during the London session, enter on a Heiken Ashi candle color flip and scale out three equal-lot orders (two at 1:1, one trailed below each new HA candle).
eligibility_reason: Fully mechanical HA color-change + 100EMA trend filter on FX majors H1 with stated SL (entry candle), TP=SL, 1% risk per order and HA-trailing exit — implementable in pure MQL5, FTMO/DXZ compatible.
notes: Forum thread, only 12 posts. Author concedes heavy whipsaw/stop-outs in flat markets. Generic HA+EMA trend signal with weak/common edge; distinguishing features are the 06:00 GMT +8-9h session window and the A/B/C 3-order scale-out MM (borrowed from Peter Crown 'The Dibs'). Pairs: EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/CHF, USD/JPY. HA color is deterministic from HA open/close. Poster cfudge suggested an HA-histogram variant using the actual candle for SL/TP to survive flats.

source_ref: Proc, "Previous Day Breakout Edge System", ForexFactory thread 1075281, ~2021, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1075281/previous-day-breakout-edge-system
concept: A confirmed break-and-close of the previous day's high (long) or low (short) on the H1 chart signals continuation, entered at the next candle's open with a fixed 1:2 R:R and an optional 34-SMA trend filter.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic prior-day-level breakout, pure price rules in MQL5, fixed SL/TP with defined risk, EURUSD/GBPUSD data available, FTMO/DXZ-compatible.
notes: Core OP rules: trading day starts 22:00 GMT (cyclic day boundary); enter at open of the H1 candle AFTER an H1 close beyond prev-day high/low; SL 12.5 pips / TP 25 pips (R:R 1:2). Optional 34-SMA filter (price above SMA for longs). Author claims ~57% WR but only with discretionary fundamental/news overlay; Maddin's mechanical M30 backtest EU 2020-2021 (1 trade/day, extended London 06:00-17:00 GMT-1, SL13/TP26) showed -3R over 13 months -> edge unproven; klw notes popular static breakout unlikely to hold. Variants (one core, treat as params to sweep at Q02): ATR-based SL/TP + breakeven (NikoArms ATR(72)); wider stops for gold/USA100 (SL20/TP30); 'false-breakout' re-entry filter (MachineLearn, judged not worth it); '3-Day-Levels' trend filter; yonnie's pre-breakout 25-35% ATR pullback entry. Reported to extend to GBPJPY, USA100/NDX, XAUUSD but best-specified on FX. Reputability: anonymous FF thread, no shared audited stats (Balke-precedent keeps it in scope). DaveBerg's late post proposes a DISTINCT inside-range mean-reversion concept (trade BETWEEN prev-day H/L, sell near prev-day-avg-to-high when weekly/monthly candle bearish) but it is VAGUE (needs unspecified patterns/news/variables) -> not separately implementable.

source_ref: LauraT, 'Daylight Trading Strategy', ForexFactory thread 1086170 (2021), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1086170/daylight-trading-strategy (TradingView port by pedrobhs referenced p.23)
concept: Take trend-continuation entries when a fast price MA and two smoothed MAs of a hidden Williams %R all separate into a confirmed gap ('daylight') and price closes back beyond the fast MA in the trend direction.
eligibility_reason: Fully mechanical entry (WPR 14 hidden + SMMA 8/21 sub-window, 5-SMMA and 5-SMMA-shift-5 on price, daylight>=~4) and deterministic exit options exist (MA cross-back or ADR/ATR target); pure MQL5, no ML, data available on .DWX.
notes: Colors: main chart Green=5-SMMA, Red=5-SMMA shifted +5; sub-window Red=8-SMMA, Blue=21-SMMA applied to Williams %R(14). Short = Red>Green with daylight on price AND price closes below Green AND Red>Blue with daylight in sub-window; long is mirror. Exit: 4 stated options (sub-window MAs slope reverse, red/green cross, red/blue cross, or ADR%/ATR-multiple target) — pick a deterministic one; author uses only a far 'emergency' stop and discretionary TP (the vague part; mechanize with cross-back exit + wide catastrophic stop). Market/TF are variants of one core: author prefers indices/gold/oil, M5 endorsed for indices, D1 usable with smaller size; M15 recommended floor for FX. Advises flat before major FX news. Generic MA-cross trend follower — no structural/limit-to-arbitrage edge (crosses our FX-edge doctrine caution), hence LOW despite clean rules. Linked-but-not-in-PDF variants: 'Aggressive Hourly', '15m Aggressive', '-50 trend filter', 'Standalone -50 Strategy' (rules absent here, cannot classify).

source_ref: wdmenet1991, "EMA's Band Scalp Trading System", ForexFactory thread 1140599, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1140599/emas-band-scalp-trading-system (single post, no date stated)
concept: M1 trend-pullback scalp: a 50-EMA high/low/close band above/below the 100-EMA defines trend direction, and a Stochastic cross out of oversold/overbought on a pullback into the band triggers entry.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 mechanical rules (EMA50 H/L/C band, EMA100, Stochastic 14/3/3), deterministic entry/exit, defined SL (3 pips beyond 50-EMA band edge) and TP (7-12 pips); FX majors M1 data via .DWX; FTMO/DXZ compatible.
notes: Single anonymous post, no track record. Pullback proximity ('rally back toward the 3 EMAs') left qualitative -> reasonable-interpretation needed, hence PARTIAL. M1 scalp with 7-12 pip TP and 2.5-pip max spread is highly cost/commission sensitive; generic trend+stoch pullback variant, not an uncorrelated event/energy/JPY/long-vol driver. Pairs: EUR/USD, GBP/USD, AUD/USD.

source_ref: sonicdeejay (Sonic) & traderathome (TAH), 'Sonic R. System', ForexFactory thread 114792, 2008 (opening post revised 2014), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/114792/sonic-r-system
concept: A 34-EMA high/low/close band (the 'wave') defines trend, and price closing beyond the band during the London/NY window triggers a buy/sell stop a few pips past the breakout candle's extreme, confirmed by MACD slope and Stochastic direction.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 mechanizable EMA-band session breakout with MACD/Stoch confirmation and hard stops (FTMO/DXZ-safe); discretionary S&R-based exits and slope/SHI-channel bias filters must be operationalized.
notes: Core = 34 EMA on High, Low and Close (the 'wave'), confirmed pg34. Trading windows 1-4AM & 7-11AM EST = London + NY overlap (pg10) - session field set to London but NY window applies too. Entry (pg10): wait for breakout candle to close beyond band, place buy/sell stop few pips beyond its high/low + MACD sloping in trade direction + Stoch pointing that way. Whipsaw filters (pg7): EMA slope angle (long 1-2 o'clock, short 4-5, avoid flat 3 o'clock ranging), multi-TF H1/D1 bias via SHI-channel trendline indicator, no-chase. Inspired by Raghee Horner 'Forex Trading for Maximum Profit'. M5 scalp and M15 swing are the same core rules on different TFs = one row. JPY-crosses (EUR/JPY) prominent in the author's examples. Extremely popular retail system -> likely crowded / edge-decayed and no structural limit-to-arbitrage argument; author states 'not 100% pure mechanical' (pg28) and exits (TP at S&R / 'M-shape broken', SL at nearest support) are discretionary. PVSRA and 'Scout' trades are referenced but detailed only in external linked PDFs (not in this thread); PVSRA is explicitly an analysis method, not an entry method.

source_ref: joyny, "Notable numbers strategy", ForexFactory thread 1182304, 2022-2024. https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1182304-notable-numbers-strategy
concept: Fade psychological repeated-digit price levels ('notable numbers' such as 00/66/444): when the prior N days' highs and lows all sit on one side of the level and price then reaches it, enter a mean-reversion trade against the approach with fixed %-of-account SL/TP.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 deterministic level/N-day-lookback/session rules on available FX majors+crosses with fixed % SL/TP, no ML/grid; but per-symbol digit and lookback were MT5-optimizer-picked (overfit risk), so framework must re-derive params, not trust the thread's.
notes: Same core rule engine parameterised per symbol; treat as ONE candidate. Entry evaluated on M5 bar opens only (author's 2023-06-23 correction); level touch is BUY-from-above / SELL-from-below (reversion). Per-symbol table (notable#/lookback-days/SL/TP): EURUSD 66/2d/0.5%/0.8%; GBPUSD 00/22d/0.4%/0.4%; USDJPY 444/20d/0.25%/0.25%; EURGBP 66/13d/0.9%/0.35%; AUDUSD 33/42d/0.55%/0.85%; USDCAD 88/9d/0.75%/0.15%; AUDNZD 333/1d/0.8%/0.35%; AUDCAD 55/43d/0.6%/0.4%; EURCAD 44/23d/0.55%/0.65%. Sessions vary per symbol (EURUSD London->NY window; GBPUSD/EURGBP Sydney/Tokyo 'calm market'; AUDUSD Tokyo-London; USDCAD around London open); author stresses entering in calm Asia sessions and letting UK traders push price off the level. Only 00/50 have a documented round-number microstructure basis; arbitrary digits (66/444/333/88) plus per-symbol lookback strongly suggest optimization artifact. Low trade counts on several legs (USDJPY 15 deals in 11yr; 100%-win legs are r/r 5:1 small-sample flattery). Params tuned on 2013-2019 in-sample + 2020-2022 forward. Single anonymous author promoting a signals site (blometech.com) + MyFxBook; reputability moderate. Secondary: Lazar83 links a YouTube round-number (50/00) EA idea but gives no complete rules (VAGUE) - not carried as its own row.

source_ref: joyny, "Notable numbers strategy" (CADJPY reverse setup post), ForexFactory thread 1182304, 2022. https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1182304-notable-numbers-strategy
concept: Inverse of the fade: when price that has spent the prior N days on one side of a notable-number level breaks through it, ride the continuation in the breakout direction with fixed %-of-account SL/TP.
eligibility_reason: Same pure-MQL5 deterministic engine as the fade but with reversed entry polarity (momentum/continuation); implementable and FTMO/DXZ-compatible, yet demonstrated on a single symbol with a small sample - likely an optimizer polarity flip.
notes: Stated only for CADJPY: notable '88' (e.g. 104.88), previous 41 days on one side, enter in the breakthrough direction (BUY when prior 41d were below and price reaches '88' from below), TP 1% / SL 0.75%, entry window 14:00-22:00 (London+2h). Distinct edge from Row 1 (continuation vs reversion) so listed separately; single-symbol evidence, so a genuine reverse edge vs. curve-fit sign flip is unresolved - let Q02-Q10 arbitrate.

source_ref: sharpiemomo, "Simple System", ForexFactory forum thread #1184099 (year not stated, n.d.), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1184099-simple-system
concept: Enter on the first opposite-color close of a custom modified-Heiken-Ashi bar (APB) as a momentum-rejection reversal scalp on US30 during the NY open, stopping at the prior swing.
eligibility_reason: Exit and sizing are wholly discretionary (author states "no TP rule", takes partials ~0.5R by instinct, scratches stalling trades by feel, sizes via subjective "clicks"); entry depends on an undisclosed proprietary "APB" (modified Heiken Ashi) indicator plus paid range bars, and setup quality is explicitly experience/instinct-based -- no mechanically complete rule set to implement.
notes: Entry kernel (Heiken-Ashi color-flip reversal, stop at prior swing high/low) is the only mechanizable part but is a classic discretionary chart pattern with no structural edge -- congruent with the closed Wyckoff/SMC/chart-pattern doctrine. Author trades US30 09:30-12:00 EST (also formerly NASDAQ/NDX), M15 and M5 plus paid range bars; risk = "Risk Per Click" ($250/click, max 4, MFR $1k). Community members (demon83, MrEnterprise) tried and failed to extract a mechanical exit; author rejected the 5-EMA-touch TP suggestion. Thread is 47 pages but content after p.4 is trade-log screenshots and Q&A with no additional rules. Uses FTMO demo for practice only.

source_ref: SwingMan, "Golden-Cross Trading-Idea", ForexFactory thread 1207682, 2025, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1207682/golden-cross-trading-idea (method draws on Dimitris Tsokakis, "Anticipating Moving Average Crossover", Technical Analysis of Stocks & Commodities, Feb 2007)
concept: Go with the trend defined by a period-5 LWMA of Close crossing a period-5 LWMA of Open, taken only in the direction of the higher-timeframe trend (Elder triple screen), entering via a stop order at the mathematically anticipated crossover price.
eligibility_reason: Core is pure-MQL5 deterministic (LWMA(5,Close) x LWMA(5,Open) cross + MTF trend filter + ATR/channel stops), FX data on .DWX, FTMO/DXZ-compatible with stops; but exact entry trigger, SL/TP multiples and MM are left to the trader.
notes: Author explicitly says entry/risk/exit and money-management are 'the job of the trader' — it is an indicator toolkit, not a finished system. Lagging MA-crossover = generic trend follower, no structural/limit-to-arbitrage edge; forum member (MickyMouseFX) flags the lag eats most of each move. Distinctive twist is placing a buy-stop/sell-stop at the predicted crossing price (Tsokakis anticipation). Suggested TP/SL = min(ATR, ChannelOsc channel range). Inherently multi-timeframe (Elder triple screen: D1-H4-H1, W1-D1-H4, H4-H1-M15 etc.); primary example trades D1 with entries on H4 in direction of W1 trend, plus incidental London-open FX entries (EURUSD/EURGBP/EURJPY/GBPJPY/USDCAD/AUDUSD). Variant mentioned by emmanuel7788: 5LWMA Close shift0 / 5EMA Typical shift+2 for signal. 'SmallWick' companion indicator is named but never given mechanical rules; no separate candidate. All artifacts are .ex4/MT4 indicators (no EA); source code not posted.

source_ref: rockzz, "Your EA v3 - Daily Low & High Strategy", ForexFactory thread 1233107 (2023), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1233107/your-ea-v3-daily-low-high (source .mq4/.ex4 attached in first post; strategy rules restated in posts #24 and #55/kai.cao)
concept: On the new daily candle, trade a breakout of the previous day's high or low, choosing direction by the longer of the previous candle's two wicks (open-to-low vs high-to-open).
eligibility_reason: Deterministic pure-MQL5 daily pending-stop breakout with SL/TP in pips; no ML/external data; FX-major+cross data on .DWX; SL+TP make it FTMO/DXZ compatible.
notes: Rules: wickBuy = prevOpen - prevLow (lower shadow), wickSell = prevHigh - prevOpen (upper shadow). If wickBuy>wickSell place BuyStop at prevHigh + PipsAboveHigh (SL prevHigh - StopLossPips); if wickSell>wickBuy place SellStop at prevLow - PipsBelowLow (SL prevLow + StopLossPips). TP=TakeProfitPips. Suggested defaults TP=100 SL=30 (~1:3 R:R); emmanuel7788 argues min 1:2. Caveats lowering priority: EA is ChatGPT-generated, OP admits it 'hits stop loss everytime' in live and warns tester results diverge; backtests were control-point not every-tick (ramobomb), skeptics flag overfit/low-winrate (AleksDark) and 'results can't be legit' (ghwatson19); tienphuc2910 disputes wick logic for bull/bear candles. Variants: v3.1 (Cunpa) adds ATR-scaled variable exits and fixes a +2pip offset bug in SL calc; kai.cao requested delete-unfilled-pending-after-1-day and close-at-daily-candle-close TP options. Also demoed on US30 CFD (Indices) by driven18, so applicable beyond FX. Sunday/Friday-candle handling matters (Erebus/emmanuel7788 note different results with/without the small Sunday candle).

source_ref: cobraforex (idea from 'Turhovach' + THV team), 'THV system, final edition', ForexFactory thread 127271 (2008-2009); deterministic EA variant coded by user 'fxid10t'. https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/127271
concept: Enter when price sits on the same side of a Hull MA, a T3 MA and the Ichimoku cloud with a matching Heiken-Ashi color, and exit when any of those confluence conditions flips.
eligibility_reason: A deterministic coded EA variant (Hull 55/0.2 + T3 8/0.04 + Ichimoku 2/5/8 + Heiken-Ashi + OsMA 12/26/7, fixed 50-pip SL) exists in-thread; pure-MQL5, no ML, EURUSD/AUDUSD M1/M5 data available via .DWX.
notes: Core system is discretionary: author refuses an EA and leans on price action, pivots, S&R, psych levels and 'feeling'; but user fxid10t posted full working MQL4 source (pages 19-20) giving a codeable entry/exit/SL rule set. Manual V4 rules (page 1) cite the THV Trix cross as the 'heart'; the coded EA substitutes OsMA and omits Trix. Confluence of lagging MAs (Hull, T3, Ichimoku, HA) with no structural edge; fajst_k flags 'many false signals', Gofer Pips and final poster report the EA overtrades ('takes too many trades... will never work'). Best-time note: London/NY overlap high-volume hours (not a session-structural rule). Variants folded in: M1 extreme-scalp vs M5; long-only above Coral / short-only below. Scalping on M1/M5 is cost-sensitive - must be tested net of spread/commission.

source_ref: Knodlz, 'Range Breakout System', ForexFactory forum thread #1299658 (undated; references JPY drop of 11 July, ~15R on the short), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1299658-range-breakout-system
concept: Straddle the Asian-session range with buy/sell stop orders at the range high/low, stop-loss on the opposite range side, no take-profit, trail the stop on new highs/lows and flatten at the NY-session close.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic session range-breakout, pure MQL5, SL on opposite range side + time-based exit (no martingale/grid, FTMO/DXZ-compatible); USDJPY/GBPUSD/NAS100 all in .DWX universe.
notes: Rules FULL only for USDJPY: range 01:00-06:00 GMT+3 (author corrected GMT vs server-time confusion -> 00:00-05:00 Berlin/UTC+2), buy-stop on range high, sell-stop on range low, SL on opposite border, no TP, delete untriggered pendings 1.5h before NY open, trail SL on highs/lows, close all at 20:00 GMT+3. Promised GBPUSD/NAS100 (Indices) variants 'differ slightly' but parameters were never posted despite repeated requests -> those extensions are VAGUE. Distinguishing feature vs a fixed-R breakout is the no-TP + trailing-stop + hard time-exit. HAZARD: range/opening-range breakout family is Balke-adjacent (repo evidence: edge real but dies at Q05-DD ~21-23%, does not generalize) -> validate against that failure mode. Single unverified forum source, no backtest, one anecdotal example ('10 years', '15R'); reputability low -> priority capped at MED despite favorable session-structure + JPY-cross fit.

source_ref: 1dominican, "Horizontal line system", ForexFactory Trading Systems forum, thread 1311677, n.d. — https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1311677/horizontal-line-system (community formalization by user rell4 in same thread; TRO / TheRumpledOne daily-open indicator lineage via kreslik.com/forums/therumpledone)
concept: Breakout-continuation once price commits a fixed pip distance beyond the daily-open reference line, betting intraday momentum keeps extending away from the open.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic daily-open stop-entry + fixed-pip SL, pure-MQL5 buildable on GBPUSD/.DWX, FTMO/DXZ-compatible; only TP is unspecified in the original (design-open — community Fibo-1.618 / trailing variants supply a concrete exit).
notes: Core rules (1dominican, p1/p15): buy stop DO+10 pips / sell stop DO-10 pips on GBPUSD (gold & US30 use 20 pips), SL ~1 pip beyond the opposite side of the line (11 pips GBPUSD / 21 pips US30), never simultaneous long+short, single-pair focus, broker-time daily open. Original TP vague ('whatever you can get'). rell4 variant (p22-23): 9-pip line, Fibo 0=SL/1=entry/1.618=TP1, wait for 2nd breakout if a signal fires before 10:30 MT4 time else take the 1st, check calendar (high-impact '3-bull' events invalidate). Silkysilv variant: 10-pip hard SL, BE+1 at +5 pips, 5-pip trailing. No backtest/stats posted; multiple posters warn of whipsaws; low-reputability single-user showcase thread. Distinct from swing-MR — a session/structure (opening-range) breakout driver. Test fixed-R and trailing exit variants.

source_ref: jamesagnew, '144 x 77 ema and 77 ema method', ForexFactory thread 1323194 (2 posts, complete), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1323194

source_ref: Sol72, 'Trading System Based on Monthly, Weekly, and Daily Opening Prices,' ForexFactory thread 1328051 (2025), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1328051/trading-system-based-on-monthly-weekly-and-daily
concept: Monthly/weekly/daily opening prices act as S/R levels; trade bounces off them (long) or rejections of them (short), filtered by RSI-50 and candlestick confirmation.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 level reaction with RSI/candle filter, deterministic once the entry trigger is pinned; D1/W1/M1 opens computable for FX/metals/indices; has SL + 1:2 RR and Friday-close fits framework.
notes: Weekly/monthly-open anchor is a genuine session-structure reference (mild differentiation from index/metal swing-MR). But 'bounce + confirmation' is subjective and needs a concrete trigger (e.g. pin-bar at open + RSI cross) before build. Variants in thread, same core level-reaction idea: flashpaul's D1 rejection-wick fade at monthly/weekly open (p.5, 31-32) and LucasFX7's enter-at-close (p.32). Example prices ~2500-2900 (gold-range); concept ports to FX/Indices.

source_ref: Sol72, 'Algorithm for Entering a Trade' (weekly-open break + order block + extreme volume), in ForexFactory thread 1328051 (2025), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1328051/trading-system-based-on-monthly-weekly-and-daily
concept: After price breaks/sweeps the weekly opening level, enter a limit order at the extreme-volume order-block candle once price closes back beyond it — a liquidity-grab reversal.
eligibility_reason: Fully specified and deterministic OB/sweep/confirmation-close/limit-entry mechanics in pure MQL5, SL beyond OB + 1:2 RR; but the core 'extreme volume' filter needs real exchange volume (only tick-volume proxy on .DWX) and this ICT/SMC order-block/liquidity-sweep family is already falsified in-house.

Strong dead-end precedent: icy-tea/first-swing/Silver-Bullet/Wyckoff-SMC all RETIRE / no mechanical edge. TP options given: next weekly/daily level, next OB, FVG midpoint, or 1:2 RR.

source_ref: Forum thread 'M1 Scalping 2B2B (2 Breaks 2 Bases)', author handle Inthebox, ForexFactory, 2025 (thread references 27feb25). https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1333089-1m-scalping-2b2b
concept: Enter with the HTF trend at a market-session open on an M1 liquidity sweep that reverses into a break-and-retest 'base' (engulfing origin) at a prior-session/HTF key level, with a fixed 40-60 pip TP and SL beyond the base.
eligibility_reason: Skeletal but mechanizable price-action rules (session-open filter, HHHL/LHLL trend, prior-session high/low sweep, engulfing 'base' entry, candle-cannot-close-beyond-base invalidation, SL beyond base, fixed 40-60 pip TP); HTF key-level and base selection need mechanical proxies (prior-day/session OHLC, fractal swings).
notes: Discretionary SMC/liquidity-sweep family: author explicitly calls key-level/break/base selection 'a judgement call' and distrusts backtesting ('testing experiment, forward testing'). Same core rules cover the aggressive 1B1B variant (1 break 1 base, trend continuation, M1 and above) and the 'MTF engulf + 1B1B combo' update -> one row. Demonstrated almost exclusively on GOLD (XAUUSD), avg 40-60 pip range; author says other pairs need their own range calibration. In-thread tight-spread/scalping cost warning (Mandariin recommends futures). Overlaps QM's already-mined gold-ORB/breakout and retired SMC/ICT chart-pattern lines -> treat as low-confidence; pipeline judges edge.

source_ref: jamesagnew, "1 hour system trade with stochastics", ForexFactory thread 1346623 (n.d.), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1346623-1-hour-system-trade-with-stochastics
concept: Enter when fast EMAs (3/5) cross the 13 EMA with a stochastic 50-level cross confirmation and fixed pip stop/target — a lagging trend-ignition crossover on H1 FX majors.
eligibility_reason: Plain lagging EMA-crossover + stochastic confirmation with no structural edge; serial low-quality poster, zero backtest data (author: 'backtesting is useless'), rules mutate across ~5 templates with no stable parameter set.
notes: Single core idea presented as 5 mutating variants (all one row): (1) base = 3/5 EMA cross 13 EMA + stochastic cross 50, SL 40 then 20 pips, TP 40 pips, all majors; (2) simplified 13-EMA open/close breakout, hold until 1H close crosses back, TP up to 80; (3) 26-EMA trail entry/exit; (4) '5x5' = 5 EMA shift 5, hold until price breaches, multi-entry scale-out 20/40/60 pips (120 total risk); (5) 5x5 high/low breakout. Community + poster's own churn signal noise; matches FX-edge doctrine of edgeless indicator crossovers. Forum source, but rejection is on absence of usable edge, not reputability.

source_ref: jamesagnew, '144 ema method', ForexFactory thread #1348501 (30 posts, 2 pages), n.d. — https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1348501-144-ema-method
concept: Enter on a 5-minute close past a 34-EMA displaced 16 bars forward, with a 144-EMA breach as the stop and a fixed 17-pip target.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic displaced-EMA breakout, pure MQL5, FX M5 data available, explicit SL (144-EMA breach) + TP (17 pips) — FTMO/DXZ compatible.
notes: Low-reputation forum source: per thread the proponent posts a new system nightly (none good) and never shows live trades; skeptics call it curve-fit MA crossover. Params arbitrary (34-EMA shift 16, 17-pip TP, 144-EMA SL) — 'why these numbers?' unanswered. Archetypal MA-crossover, flagged non-robust by our own FX-edge doctrine. Variant (page 2): drop the 17-pip TP, hold until opposite 34-EMA close. Secondary ref by flashpaul to a sibling '144 x 77 ema and 77 ema method' thread.

source_ref: jamesagnew, 'trade fractals, this method is a winner', ForexFactory thread 1348734 (forum discussion, 41 posts), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1348734/trade-fractals-this-method-is-a-winner
concept: Breakout 5 pips past a confirmed Bill Williams fractal - buy above a down-fractal swing low, sell below an up-fractal swing high - with a fixed 2:1 reward:risk.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic fractal-breakout, fixed SL 50 / TP 100 pips, H4 FX (USDCAD) data via .DWX, FTMO/DXZ-compatible; forum author admits not trading/testing and it is a generic breakout, hence low priority.
notes: Base rule: down fractal -> buy stop at signal-candle high +5 pips; up fractal -> sell stop at low -5 pips; TP 100 / SL 50 pips; H4 USDCAD, 'works on any pair'. Bill Williams fractal only confirms after 2 closed bars each side, so entry must arm on the confirmed fractal to avoid the repaint that FerruFx flags. Optional confirmation-candle filter variant (page 6): take buy only if the candle right of a down fractal is higher-high+higher-low, sell only if lower-high+lower-low - fold as a parameter, not a separate row. Thread widely panned as untested spam (author has ~58 similar threads). Secondary idea by perth1985 (Heikin-Ashi 1:1 RR with MA/trend confirmation from an external video) is too VAGUE to classify - not a candidate.

source_ref: "Never Lose Again" thread by Erebus (with kleybenny, driven18, notabdullah1, T721), ForexFactory thread 1371892, 2025-2026, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1371892/never-lose-again
concept: Break of the previous week's high/low in the direction of the prevailing weekly bias, held as a swing with SL at the recent swing point and all positions flat by Friday close.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 prior-week high/low breakout with a stop and Friday-flat exit; FX/metals data on .DWX; discretionary overlay (bias, swing SL, ADR TP moves) must be mechanized.
notes: Entry anchor (prior-week high/low breakout) and Friday-close exit are objective; SL 'at recent swing point', 'weekly bias', and 'move TP daily using ADR targets' are explicitly discretionary (author: 'not a system with hard entries', 'individual discretion'). Risk <=2%/position, initial RR 1:1 scalable to daily/weekly/monthly ADR targets, later variants 3:1 + trailing stop. Maps directly onto the already-explored Balke weekly-range-breakout family (a poster even calls it 'Good Ole Range Breakout... for a weekly as a range') which the shop found edge-real but Q05-DD-fatal and non-generalizing -> low novelty. Live evidence is a small ~3-week journal; author repeatedly flags Gold as the losing leg (FX-only did better). Attached 'Previous Week High Low.mq4' only plots the levels, not a strategy. Secondary forum chatter (Dow Futures 5m breakout, 'go opposite gold breakouts') are undefined asides, not separate candidates. Forum-source reputability caveat.

source_ref: Voldemar227, "VR Gap Open Source Trading Strategy", ForexFactory thread 1394867 (single post), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/1394867 ; open-source EA also published on MQL5 Code Base (MT4: https://www.mql5.com/en/code/9994 , MT5: https://www.mql5.com/en/code/72239 ).
concept: At each new bar open, if the absolute difference between the previous bar's close and the current bar's open exceeds a threshold, fade the impulse (buy a down-gap, sell an up-gap) betting price returns to close the gap.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic rule-based gap-fade: entry (gap > MinGapSize, fade direction), fixed points SL, TP = gap size; pure MQL5, no indicators, no ML, has stops, FTMO/DXZ compatible.
notes: Single distinct edge = gap-fill / session-structure return driver, uncorrelated with index/metal swing-MR. On continuous quotes close[1] approx open[0], so bar-to-bar gaps effectively only fire at liquidity breaks: weekend re-opens (FX/Metals) and overnight session gaps (cash indices on D1) - hence market=Indices/timeframe=D1 as best fit though author claims any symbol/any TF; trade frequency likely low, watch Q02 >=5/yr floor. TP=gap-size and fixed points SL will need per-instrument tuning; author warns not for news/high-vol without adjusting MinGapSize. Fixed-lot sizing must be replaced by framework RISK_FIXED. Source is a single-post code-dump by an unknown handle (low reputability) advertising an existing open-source MQL5 EA - lowers priority, does not reject.

source_ref: Everett et al., 'Parabolic SAR robot (modified)', ForexFactory thread 142161 (18 posts, complete; 'Happy New Year' opening post, year not stated in excerpt, thread id implies ~2011), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/142161
concept: Trend-following entry on a Parabolic SAR flip (price crossing to the opposite side of the SAR dot), ride the move with a trailing stop; author reports the short side flips work better than longs.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5, deterministic single-indicator system on EURUSD (data available), no ML, stops + Friday-close fit FTMO/DXZ; but entry-only precise while trailing-stop distance and SL are unquantified in the base EA.
notes: Explicit entry (p.3): SELL when price<SARCurrent AND price>SARPrevious; BUY when price>SARCurrent AND price<SARPrevious (i.e. on the SAR flip). Exit = trailing stop; base EA sets no hard SL ('handled internally'), author advises adding a moderate SL. Original setup = EURUSD M30, default SAR settings; enum has no M30 so recorded as H1, which the thread itself recommends as the minimum TF to cut whipsaw. Proposed variants (all same core, mention only): higher-TF SAR confirmation (take sells only if 4h+daily SAR agree), MA trend filter, buy/sell-limit straddle entry +/-5 pips of the flip bar, lock-in-pips trailing (13->5, 25->15), session start/stop filter, Friday close-out. Vanilla PSAR flip with no structural edge, author admits 'modest, not consistent' results and members note heavy whipsaw in ranges; forum-anecdotal, so LOW. Martingale conversion suggested by one poster is NOT adopted and would be incompatible (a)-(d).

source_ref: ForexFactory thread "How you place your stop loss?", started by yyc196, ForexFactory forum, undated, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/148885 (30 posts)
concept: A stop-loss placement / money-management opinion thread with no complete deterministic entry+exit strategy defined by any contributor.
eligibility_reason: Discussion of stop-loss philosophy and money management only; no post specifies a full deterministic entry+exit system, so nothing is implementable as a strategy.
notes: Notable fragments: (1) ixbone runs a manual martingale (add to losers, doubling lots, SL removed on final legs) on vague indicator entries -> incompatible with FTMO/DXZ DD limits, REJECTED as martingale/stops-optional. (2) mikkom: 'daily breakout methods' with 2-day-ATR initial stop + Donchian/last-week's-low trail -> the only edge-adjacent idea, but entry is entirely undefined (fails deterministic-entry rule). (3) TJPLD: EUR/USD H4, 50-70 pip stop behind S/R, no TP, move to BE at +50 pips -> exit/stop management only, no entry rule. (4) Dr.Geppynius/Xaron/gspajon/birdt: 1% risk, stop-defines-size, anti-martingale sizing, structure trailing -> MM/exit heuristics, no entries. Thread yields zero usable candidate strategies.

source_ref: Thierry89, "RSI & CCI system", ForexFactory thread 149331, 2009, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/149331
concept: Go long/short when a fast oscillator (RSI) and a trend/momentum indicator (CCI) simultaneously confirm the same directional bias at bar close.
eligibility_reason: Entry fully deterministic (RSI8>50 & CCI14>0 buy / RSI8<50 & CCI14<0 sell on candle close); exit named (reverse signal / trailing stop / TP) but stop and target distances unspecified.
notes: Pairs EUR/USD, USD/CHF, EUR/JPY, GBP/USD (touches JPY cross). Best during European/London session (arubin, p17). Variants (all same core, one row): RSI 55/45 no-trade band instead of 50 (DirkH143 p4-5); RSI period 14 vs 8; CCI threshold +/-100 instead of 0 and CCI 89/21 or 170/34 (ClearTrade p16); ADX(14)>20 chop filter (arubin p17); 200-EMA trend filter (Zuke p5); H4/M30/M5 MTF confirmation (samer960 p19). Forum anecdote only ("400 pips in two days"), no backtest; author trades manually and declines EA. Weak edge fit: RSI and CCI are redundant momentum measures (arthurb p27: "they tell the same story") -> effectively a single-factor momentum crossover, low orthogonality.

source_ref: SteveHopwood, "AshFX system, V2: Steve Hopwood's trading robot", ForexFactory Thread 166910 (2009), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/166910 - core Ash V2 manual rules in companion thread https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/156889
concept: A Parabolic SAR flip on the trading timeframe, confirmed by a higher-timeframe trend filter (MACD, PSAR or 64 SMA) plus stochastic, is harvested with an OZFX-style 5-order scale-out and a trailing runner.
eligibility_reason: Standard indicators (PSAR/MACD/Stochastic/MA) implementable in pure MQL5 on FX majors + JPY crosses; carries a fixed SL so the 5-order scale-out is not martingale; but the full Ash V2 entry confluence is deferred to companion thread 156889.
notes: Generic multi-indicator trend-following confluence; forum-amateur provenance with unverified '10,000 pips/week' claims and no backtest evidence. Pairs cited: EURUSD/GBPUSD/USDJPY/USDCHF + GBPJPY/EURJPY/EURCHF; also run on H1. Exit params (5-digit points): TP=2500, SL=1250, BreakEven=2000@200, JumpingStop(trailing)=1000, MinPipsToNextSupport=300; break-even at ~50 pips, 4 orders scale out in 50-pip steps, 5th is a runner. V1.5 variant swaps MACD for a 64-SMA regime filter (long only above, short only below) - same core, one row. FFCal news filter replaceable by framework news calendar. Full AO/AC/stochastic entry rules live in companion manual thread 156889, not this PDF.

source_ref: brue, '5min Bollinger breakout system' (a.k.a. the 'Bands Breakout' MT4 EA), ForexFactory Thread 19073, 2007, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/19073-5min-bollinger-breakout-system
strategy_name: EURUSD M5 BB-touch trend breakout (ADX+DeMarker gate, 10xATR stop)
concept: In a strong trend (ADX>=40, DeMarker at an extreme), enter in the breakout direction when 5-min EURUSD price comes within 5 pips of a Bollinger band, scalping a fixed 20-pip target behind a very wide 10x-H4-ATR catastrophic stop.
eligibility_reason: Apparent profitability is a documented in-thread artifact of invalid in-bar M5 tick backtesting on poor IBFX data (linear equity curves flagged by posters) plus a ~20:1 tight-20pip-TP / wide-250-500pip-SL profile; author states any risk-sane (smaller) stop 'loses lots of money', so no risk-compatible edge survives and the only profitable form breaches FTMO daily-loss / DXZ 5% DD at its 10% VAR sizing.
notes: Not martingale/grid/HFT (single position, real stop present) so rejected on edge/(d)-compatibility grounds, not those. Indicators: BB(14,2SD; low-price upper band, high-price lower band), DeMarker(14), ADX(14, typical price), ATR(H4,100), EMA(14) profit-exit (long exit = profitable & close<EMA; short exit = profitable & close>EMA); VAR-based lot sizing. Variants in-thread are the same core rules: fixed-stoploss version, fixed-lot version, period=10 tweak, int->double lot fix -- one row. Many users report the EA 'does not trade at all' (very restrictive gate), and critics (Skyline, Craig) diagnosed the >20:1 R/R as an eventual account-blower. Classic retail EURUSD indicator combo -- not an uncorrelated return driver.

source_ref: 'Trading with Deadly Accuracy' by user wmd (wmd4x), ForexFactory thread 206723 (n.d., c. 2010); https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/206723-trading-with-deadly-accuracy
concept: After a breakout, a support/resistance level that was breached but never retested becomes an 'untested' level where resting orders accumulate; trade the eventual return to that level expecting it to hold (broken resistance turns support, or vice versa).
eligibility_reason: Mechanizable in pure MQL5 (pivot-based S/R + breakout / untested-retest detection) with a defined SL (10x spread) and half-off-at-1:1 exit; FX/metals data available, hard stops present so FTMO/DXZ compatible; downgraded because level and 'failed parabola' identification is eyeball/discretionary.
notes: Entry: aggressive = pending order AT the untested level; conservative = wait for a to-the-pip bounce then enter with SL 10x spread beyond the level. Exit: close half at 1:1 RnR or the next S/R (whichever first), trail the rest, kill only on a level breach. SL table (10x spread): EURUSD 20-25, GBPUSD 30-40, EURJPY 35-50, GBPJPY 60-80, USDCAD 40-50 pips. Examples mostly AUDUSD, Gold/XAUUSD, GBPUSD, NZDUSD, EURUSD on H1+H4; author says news 'tends not to affect the outcome' (pure technical). Caveats: wmd/wmd4x sells a trading room + 50h of videos (wmd4x.com) and frames it as '100% accuracy' marketing hype. Thread also teases but does NOT give rules for sub-methods (diagonal/trend-line day-trade, reversal patterns, 'SAM / Simple Accuracy Method') - no harvestable rules in this excerpt.

source_ref: Brb-Fraudin (thread starter), 'Simple scalping system, all red or all green', ForexFactory thread 215160 (year not stated in excerpt; ~60 posts, first 3 pages captured), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/215160
strategy_name: Multi-TF candle-color alignment scalp (all red / all green)
concept: When the last CLOSED candles of M5, M15, M30 and H1 are all the same color, enter in that direction as a short momentum/trend-continuation scalp.
eligibility_reason: Entry fully deterministic (four closed TF candles same color); exit is semi-discretionary (author closes manually ~+15/+20 pips, SL -15) but trivially mechanizable to fixed TP/SL; pure MQL5, FX data on .DWX, has stops -> FTMO/DXZ compatible.
notes: Decision cadence anchored at H1 close (evaluate the just-closed M5/M15/M30/H1); 'closed candle' reading is the only mechanizable one -- the active-candle version is degenerate (at the top of each hour all TFs share the same M5 candle => random hourly signal, per Misiacyk/magnumfreak). Naive candle-color momentum with no limit-to-arbitrage structure; multiple experienced posters report it fails live (bobsmith: retracement stops you out before TP; finally/jonirrenicus: 'does not work'). Variants folded here: Vantage 'follow-the-1hr' filter with M5 8-LWMA cross + higher-low entry; 4xProfessor adds M1 same-color confirmation. Attached indicators are MQL4 only (All_GreenOrRed.mq4, Multi Meter Candle Color.ex4, bar_close_alarm_V1.mq4) -- reference, not usable in V5. Overlaps existing dead chart-pattern/candle-momentum FX doctrine; cheap to backtest but expected to die early (Q02/Q04).

source_ref: mer071898, "A Simple London Breakout", ForexFactory thread 230640 (2010), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/230640-a-simple-london-breakout
strategy_name: London Open Range Breakout (box + stop orders)
concept: Session-open range breakout: draw a box over the 03:00-06:00 GMT pre-Frankfurt-open range on M15, place buy/sell stop orders at the box edges (or just-inside fib-extension levels), take profit equal to the box size, stop loss at the opposite box edge, and skip days when the box exceeds ~40-50 pips.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic session-open range breakout, pure-MQL5 implementable with a hard SL and fixed TP; FX majors/crosses available via .DWX; FTMO/DXZ compatible (defined stops, Friday-close exists).
notes: Session-structure driver (favored over index/metal swing-MR) and JPY-crosses included, but a heavily-traded pattern with only marginal demonstrated edge. Best pair GBP/USD (cleanest London open after quiet Asian hours); also EUR/USD, USD/JPY, EUR/JPY, USD/CHF, EUR/GBP. In-thread backtest (xmph, GBP/USD 2009, 0.1 lot, 1 trade/day, no entry after 5pm): 251 trades, PF 1.14, 60% win, max DD 17.25% -- but 2010 YTD was a net loss (3000->2700). Variants of same core rules (ONE candidate): simple box-edge breach without fibs (Uberscalper), SL at box middle for smaller losses (nightyhawk), 07:00-10:00 GMT window + 161.8-fib TP (stringfx), 84-EMA trend filter (ingmarforex). Optional martingale-on-losers is mentioned but explicitly risky -- exclude it (violates FTMO/DXZ DD rules); base rules keep a hard SL. Box is anchored to GMT so broker-time/DST offset must be handled (poster is GMT+1, uses 04:00-07:00). Close open trades before the next box (~03:00 GMT).

source_ref: ForexFactory thread #242787 'Simple 1 EMA Strategy on M15', started by user Feliks S. (~2010), 45 posts across 3 pages, forexfactory.com; https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/242787-simple-1-ema-strategy-on-m15 . Core idea attributed by the author to 'The Outsider Method' article.
concept: After price closes across a 9-EMA, enter on the pullback candle that stays a fixed pip gap off the MA and closes beyond the prior candle's extreme, riding trend continuation at a fixed 2:1 reward-to-risk.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic MA-pullback breakout implementable in pure MQL5 with stated entry, SL (prior candle extreme +1pip+spread) and TP (2xSL); no ML/external data; but low reputability (retail forum, no verified backtest).
notes: Core: 9-EMA on GBPUSD M15; buy when price closes above EMA AND pullback candle low >=~5 pips from EMA AND its close > prior candle high (sell mirror); no trade if 3-4 candles pass after cross, if SL ~10 pips, if candle gaps open, or if MA is too flat/horizontal. Variants (same core edge, one row): (1) 14-SMA multi-timeframe version - H4 & H1 close above 14-SMA as trend filter, M15 pullback below/back above 14-SMA, 40 SL / 120 TP, breakeven at +40, close half at +80 and trail rest by 40 pips (p13); (2) gonso's EUR/USD 1m London-open session variant 0800-1000 CET, drops the pip-gap requirement, skips hammer/doji/oversized candles (p14). Prodi's SMA + MTF-MACD + RSI system mentioned but too vague to spec. Fuzzy secondary filters (MA slope/angle, 'large candle', gap-open) need quantifying. Author's own friend and two other posters (finally, roundrock's skepticism) reported no usable edge; only anecdotal manual-trade screenshots, no logged statement.

source_ref: ashraf999, 'A simple strategy with no indicators', ForexFactory thread 245777 (c. 2010), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/245777-a-simple-strategy-with-no-indicators
concept: A completed daily candle whose real body exceeds the bodies of the prior two candles, prints opposite in color to the preceding candle and closes beyond it, is expected to carry momentum into the next day toward round-number (00/50) levels.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic pure price-action D1 pattern on FX majors/crosses, no indicators/ML; entry fully specified but TP/SL at 'psychological 00/50 levels' is author-admitted subjective and needs a deterministic round-level mechanization.
notes: Entry rules (long): body[1] > body[2] AND body[1] > body[3]; candle[2] bearish; close[1] > close[2]; enter at open of next daily candle after signal completes. Short = mirror (candle[2] bullish, close[1] < close[2]). One trade per pair per day max; use last full D1 candle, skip partial Sunday candle; broker-day close is decisive (author uses FX Solutions, 21:00 GMT day start). Claimed ~7/10 hit rate but self-described untested (~2 weeks live) — forum source, low reputability lowers priority. Plain FX daily momentum/continuation, not an uncorrelated return driver; overnight hold => FX swap injection matters. VARIANTS (same core edge, note only): (1) cmac0351 drops the two-candle-body rule, uses only the longer real body, SL=75 pips, no fixed TP, trails stop to 2/3 winning-candle length daily, closes on adverse daily close; (2) fguru multi-TF confirmation: D1 signal + same formation on H4/H1 before entry. Not martingale/grid; explicit stops used.

source_ref: jaws810 et al., '3,6,9 EMA system', ForexFactory thread 252779 (17 posts, 2011-era), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/252779-369-ema-system
concept: EMA3 crossing above (buy) or below (sell) both EMA6 and EMA9 signals fast-timeframe momentum entries, exiting on a hard SL, an opposite EMA3 cross, or a small fixed TP.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic MQL5-implementable EMA crossover on FX majors/crosses with a hard SL and FTMO/DXZ-compatible risk; but a naked M5 crossover is very low edge plausibility.
notes: Entry: EMA3 crosses top of both EMA6+EMA9 = long, bottom = short; enter after close of the cross candle. Exit: 20-pip disaster SL, primary exit = opposite EMA3-cross of the 6+9, TP ~10 pips (stated as 2.5% of balance on OANDA). Variants/filters suggested in thread (all same core rules): (1) skip signals when 3/6/9 EMAs are bunched together, wait for separation; (2) use higher TF (15M/30M) for separation then wait for 5M retrace + re-cross; (3) ADX/ATR filter; (4) MACD 8/17/9 or RSI-divergence confirmation; (5) golden1 uses 6/36 EMA on M5. Author admits it was UNTESTED at posting; multiple replies warn of whipsaws/blowups in ranging markets. Forum source of low reputability -> priority lowered. TP<SL structure needs commission/spread scrutiny at Q02/Q04.

source_ref: whatfx et al., "Best trading systems on forexfactory" (ForexFactory thread 265214, ~2010-2011), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/265214/best-trading-systems-on-forexfactory
concept: A survey/opinion thread pointing to other systems by name/link with no mechanical rules of its own.
eligibility_reason: Pure meta-discussion: lists/links other threads (Nanningbob 4HR, The Dance/Choros, Vegas Tunnel, DIBS, THV, Igrok, mouteki, fozzy, SRDC 2, Philip Nel, Sonic R, 'daily 95 pips') and trading psychology chatter; no entry/exit/params stated for any strategy in this thread.
notes: Only quasi-rules present: Ronald Raygun coin-toss (heads long/tails short + discretionary manage = random entry, not a system) and a passing note that 'zaman's 2 lines' = 9pm close +/-30 pips (signal-line construction, no exit). The named referenced systems would each need to be harvested from their own source threads, not this one.

source_ref: SteveHopwood et al., 'Trading EA shell' thread, ForexFactory forum, 2011, thread #282290, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/282290-trading-ea-shell-by-stevehopwood ; the strategy passage was posted by member 'newark18'.
concept: Place a stop order beyond an H4 engulfing candle that forms on the trend-correct side of the 50-period SMA, riding continuation; hard-stop at the engulfing candle's opposite extreme and close when an H4 candle closes back across the SMA.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic engulfing + SMA-filter stop-breakout, pure-MQL5 implementable on FX H4 (.DWX), with a hard SL and an SMA-close exit; martingale/grid free, so FTMO/DXZ DD-compatible.
notes: Long: candle[2] bearish, candle[1] bullish engulfing with close>SMA50 -> BUYSTOP at high of engulfing candle, SL at its low. Short is the mirror (SELLSTOP at low, SL at high). TP optional in pips; primary exit is 'close all buys when an H4 candle closes below the SMA, all sells when it closes above'. Posted by forum member 'newark18' (not the thread author) while asking for coding help - the SMA-close exit was stated in words but left commented/unfinished in the buggy posted code (order-modify error 130). Classic candlestick-pattern trigger, a chart-pattern class repeatedly falsified in QM research, so low-conviction. The thread itself is a generic EA code 'shell' (trade-management scaffold: hidden SL/TP 'Stealth', 'Caterpillar' scale-in, 'CBI' multi-trade module, AllAverages trend indicator, Hanover module) with no standalone edge -> not a strategy candidate.

source_ref: eelfranz, "Trading Made Simple", ForexFactory thread 291622 (2011), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/291622-trading-made-simple ; a simplified variation of Dean Malone's Synergy/TDI method (compassfx.com).
concept: Momentum crossover: go with the trade when the TDI fast price line (green) crosses its signal line (red) at the open of a fresh Heiken-Ashi candle, in the direction confirmed by Stochastic(8,3,3).
eligibility_reason: Deterministic TDI green/red crossover entry (candle 1-2 only, not 3+) plus swing-high/low stop is pure-MQL5 implementable, no ML; but the exit ('green TDI goes flat / hooks over / makes a check-mark reversal') is discretionary and TDI parameters are only implied by a '5-MA TDI' template, so it needs mechanization.
notes: Single core method; variants folded in: 'bounce trade' (re-enter when green bounces off red and continues) and Dean-Malone Synergy channel stop (SlushFund: stop on the opposite 5-EMA of a two-5-EMA channel). Setup is CROSS, not merely green touching red. Direction rule: TDI rising=long, falling=short. source_ref: Kathy Lien & Boris Schlossberg, "Do the Right Thing - Trade Breakouts" (Investopedia CCI article), reposted in ForexFactory thread 325369 by Forexcube, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/325369/tasayc-system

source_ref: the_guvnor (Dean Simpson), "50 +/- MACD 4hour" (My Trading plan 50+/-), ForexFactory thread 33362, 2007, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/33362/50-macd-4hour
concept: Go long/short when the 4H MACD(5,13,1) main-line value has moved by at least +/-0.00050 between the just-closed bar and the bar two periods (8h) earlier, checked at fixed clock times.
eligibility_reason: Fully mechanical H4 MACD momentum on GBPUSD; pure MQL5, no ML, deterministic entry + fixed SL/TP exits; GBPUSD via .DWX; FTMO/DXZ-compatible (author's 5%/trade risk is a tunable param, dial down).
notes: Entry: at 0800/1200/1600/2000 (broker/local clock) evaluate MACD(5,13,1) main value of the just-closed 4H bar vs the value 2 bars earlier (the 'bar B vs bar D' clarification on p.9); |diff|>=0.00050 -> trade in that direction; only if no position open. Exit: two lots, Pos A SL30/TP30, Pos B SL30/TP45, move B to breakeven once A hits TP; positions run to SL/TP; opposite-direction next signal = reverse, same-direction = hold. Variants (same core rules, param tweaks -> one row): (1) noise filter, skip signals with |diff|<0.00150, author reports it removes >half the losers, gnllr 2007 backtest Jan+333/Feb+38/Mar+68/Apr+720 pips; (2) billbss single-lot optimization SL100/TP60, min diff 0.00038, ~+3540 pips over 5/2006-6/2007. Multiple independent forum backtests reported profitable (mild positive), but hand-backtests only, no verified equity curve. Descriptive-only asides NOT candidates: discretionary double-bottom/cup-and-handle chart-pattern overlays (VAGUE, author preferred numeric rules); The Fxorce's ZeroLag-MACD+Heikin-Ashi EA (reported unprofitable); a 'completely new system' the author teases at the end but never describes. Author's optional 'double up after X losses' 10% idea = martingale and was rejected within the thread (coin-has-no-memory argument) and by the author himself; NOT part of the eligible core. Concept is a generic acknowledged derivative of Phillip Nel's 4H-MACD strategy -> MED not HIGH; it is an FX-major momentum driver, not a prioritized uncorrelated theme.

source_ref: Olaha (thread starter), 'Simple Trading system for Intraday/short term', ForexFactory thread 33615, 2007, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/33615-simple-trading-system-for-intradayshort-term

eligibility_reason: Open-anchored pending-stop breakout with SL=open and staged TPs is pure-MQL5 deterministic, FX majors data available, has hard stops (FTMO/DXZ-compatible); forum-sourced with no stats and GBPUSD-tuned static pip offsets lower priority.
notes: Core is fully mechanical: Buy Stop at Open+34, SL=Open, TP1=Open+89, TP2=Open+144 (mirror for Sell Stop at Open-34); OCO flip on fill (if buy fills, reset sell to Open with SL=former buy level). Confirmation filters (SMA70 trend, RSI21>50/<50, Stochastic 15,3,3 above 60 buy / below 30 sell, EMA5&12 S/R) applied 'when order triggered' -- chart TF for these is ambiguous (hence PARTIAL). Move SL to +3 after 20 pips (breakeven). News filter: close 5-15 min before releases. Static pip offsets are cable-tuned; JPY-cross variants given (USDJPY 21/34/55/89, GBPJPY 34/55/89/144) and need per-symbol pip scaling. A discretionary 'trendline-as-fib' S/R tip and a manual retrace re-entry ('after price retraces, re-enter at TP1') are mentioned but not mechanized. Session-structure / open-range breakout class = uncorrelated driver vs index/metal swing-MR; forum-breakout precedent = live Balke EA.

source_ref: TheThing (handle 'Dimy'), 'Simple Daily System', ForexFactory thread 38981 (2007), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/38981-simple-daily-system — poster states he is not the author, only a follower; idea attributed to the forex-tsd.com community.
concept: Daily-bar trend continuation: three consecutive same-direction candles (by open/close vs prior day), optionally with 20>50 EMA and positive slope, trigger an entry on the next daily open with a two-step scale-out.
eligibility_reason: Fully mechanical daily FX rules (entry/stop/TP/scale-out all stated); pure MQL5, no ML, DXZ/.DWX FX daily data available; fixed-pip stop is FTMO/DXZ DD-compatible.
notes: Two variants of ONE core rule: (A) original requires 20/50 EMA(close) alignment + positive slope on both; (B) later 'NO Indicators' version (p7/p13) drops the EMA filter. Entry on OPEN of 4th daily candle after 3 consecutive days of Close>Open (long) / Close<Open (short). Candle qualification later clarified as higher open AND higher close vs prior day (not merely a bullish/bearish body). Stop = 2 pips beyond prior candle OR 90 pips, 'whichever is lower' (wording ambiguous; poster clarifies he uses ~80-90 pip fixed stop). TP1 30 pips (close half, move SL->BE), TP2 100 pips; per-pair TP tuning suggested (GBP/USD 39/80 or 20/60). Opens 2 lots. No time-based exit — only SL/TP closes. Ignore Sunday candle. Daily open = 00:00 GMT+2 broker time. JPY-cross tilt (GBP/JPY, CAD/JPY, EUR/JPY best in self-tests). Evidence = unverified 2007 self-reported paper trading; forum source lowers conviction; an in-thread member (Ebenst) noted <50% continuation after 3 consecutive daily bars.

source_ref: shovon (handle), 'Multiple & Modified Trading System', ForexFactory thread 390300 (forum discussion, undated), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/390300 — SMA-trend + engulfing/signal-candle swing system; idea credited to FF member '60 minuteman'.
concept: Enter a bullish/bearish engulfing (or a defined range-close signal candle) in the direction of a 10/20/50 SMA trend stack, sizing reward:risk to how strongly the SMAs align.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic SMA-trend + engulfing/range-close signal candle with defined SL and per-trend RR TP on FX majors/crosses (D1/H4); pure-MQL5, no ML, FTMO/DXZ-compatible.
notes: One strategy only; thread pages 5-7 and 9-11 duplicate 1-3 (pagination repeat); pages 4/8/12/13 are off-topic fundamentals chatter. Trend classes: overall = price vs 50 SMA; perfect = 10 SMA above/below both 20 & 50; temporary = 10 SMA between 20 & 50. Entry next-candle-open after signal candle; SL 1-2 pip beyond signal or prior candle extreme; TP keyed to trend (perfect 1:2, overall 1:1, temporary 2:1), 2% risk. Alternate signal candle precisely defined: close in top/bottom 25% of range AND its (or prior candle's) extreme = highest-high/lowest-low of last 5. Author excludes AUD/USD and commodities. PARTIAL: trend-direction filter stated as 'advised' not a hard rule, leaving one degree of freedom on which trend condition must hold to enter. Generic MA+engulfing pattern class (chart-pattern edges largely dead per FX-edge doctrine); low-reputability forum source, self-admitted inexperienced author, unverified '100% success' claim. Weekly/H4 are variants of the same core rules.

source_ref: MIH Bobby, 'EMA Fibonacci Level Trading Strategy', ForexFactory thread 416962 (14 posts, complete), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/416962-ema-fibonacci-level-trading-strategy
concept: Enter on the open of the next candle when price closes above/below (or bounces off) EMA(20) while RSI(10) crosses its 50 line and Stochastic(10,3,3) exits an OB/OS zone, taking profit at Fibonacci-offset levels (0.382/0.618) around the EMA.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 indicator confluence (EMA/RSI/Stoch) with stated SL/TP; FTMO/DXZ-compatible, but the 'trending vs sideway' regime filter and 'bounce back from EMA' entry are undefined.
notes: Generic MA+RSI+Stoch indicator-soup momentum combo with no structural edge and no correlated-driver fit (not event/energy/JPY/long-vol/session). Author's 'Fibonacci levels' are just 0.382/0.618 offset lines added to the EMA 'Level' tab. No backtest or trading results posted; thread replies (Davit, 4xplosion) themselves debunk OB/OS timing as lagging and suggest divergence instead. Author states M5/M15/H1 all viable (M15 chosen as central). TP variants: EMA-0.382 level or 0.618 level; SL 5-10 pips beyond EMA or 0.382 level for 1:2 RR. Low forum reputability.

source_ref: muhiuddin (handle), "Best Trading System (Only Momentum)" [MOMENTUM PRICE SYSTEM], ForexFactory thread 423512, forums.forexfactory.com, n.d. — https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/423512/best-trading-system-only-momentum
concept: Trade regular price/Momentum(28) divergence: enter on the close after momentum breaks its intra-divergence extreme (point F), betting the divergence resolves into a reversal.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic entry/exit/TP once divergence is fixed, pure-MQL5, stops + defined DD (FTMO/DXZ-compatible); but divergence-line identification is subjective and needs a mechanized swing/pivot heuristic.
notes: Rules: Momentum(28,close) on H4-D1. Bearish setup=price HH vs momentum LH; mark highest price as C, lowest momentum as F; short 2 lots on bar-close when F is penetrated. Bullish=mirror. SL at price C. TP1=|C-entry| (exit 1 lot, SL->BE), TP2=2xTP1 (trail 50% of profit each new candle), TP3=4xTP1 (trail 75%). Divergence swing lookback 8-28 bars. Author claims FX majors+crosses (incl JPY crosses: CADJPY/GBPJPY) + crude oil (XTIUSD), untested gold/silver; anecdotal forward-test only, no backtest, self-reported ~3.5-10%/mo with 32% DD, and multiple posters note different brokers show different momentum values -> divergence non-reproducible. Variant: pampaq applied same divergence idea to CCI14 on M15/M60 (same core, not a separate row). homealbum's EMA(21,8)+Bollinger 'price action' comment and KnSquare's Donchian breakout aside are too vague to be candidates. If built, the divergence detector (pivot-based regular-divergence, F=momentum extreme break confirmation) is the load-bearing mechanization risk.

source_ref: thomaas, 'Price Action made simple with Supply and Demand levels', ForexFactory thread 452780, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/452780-price-action-made-simple-with-supply-and-demand (author's free companion site www.21pips.com; method credited to Sam Seiden S&D videos and other FF threads)
concept: A consolidation 'base' preceding a strong impulse move is an unfilled supply/demand zone; a fresh first retest of that zone reverses price, entered with a limit order for a fixed risk:reward.
eligibility_reason: Core mechanic (impulse+base zone detection, fresh-only limit retest, SL beyond distal edge, fixed 1:2-1:3 TP) is deterministic and pure-MQL5 buildable, but the 'filter bad levels' and 'precisely draw levels' sections are [To come] and never delivered, so departure-strength / base-size thresholds must be parametrized by us.
notes: Zone types DBD/DBR/RBR/RBD; author says RBD and DBR are best. Filters: freshness (first touch only), strong departure (2-3 big same-colour candles), few candles in base. Entry at proximal edge, SL few pips beyond distal edge, TP fixed 1:2 or 1:3, risk 2.5%, pending orders, ~1-2 trades/wk. Primarily EUR/USD H1 but author claims any pair/TF and specifically notes gold (XAUUSD) works well -> testable on FX majors/crosses + XAUUSD. LOW priority: this is a classic support/resistance / S&D chart-pattern mean-reversion, the exact family the FX-edge doctrine (07-16) and Wyckoff/SMC closure (07-19/20) treat as structurally dead; brings no uncorrelated return driver. Variant in-thread (FerruFx #47): wait for a reversal pattern + momentum-break confirmation at the zone instead of a blind limit -> same core, fold into this row. redteamgo's discretionary crude/NG order-flow scaling is not mechanizable and not a separate candidate.

source_ref: 'The Dance Continues' (revival of 'The Dance' method by eclayf / 'Clay'), ForexFactory thread 460041, started by user '.Hawk.', c.2014 — https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/460041 (original concept thread t=236210). Rules taken from attached 'The Dance Manual.pdf' / 'The M15 Dance Manual.pdf' and .Hawk.'s in-thread trade posts.
strategy_name: The Dance — Two-Bar Touch Trade (M15 MA pullback continuation)
concept: In an established MA-stacked trend, enter with the trend when price races back in no more than two large bars to 'touch' a moving average (10 EMA, or the 35 SMA/50 EMA cluster) sloping at a steep angle, betting the pullback resumes the trend.
eligibility_reason: Core subset (3 MAs, trend stack = price+10 EMA on one side of 35/50, retrace-to-touch, fixed ~9-pip SL/TP or bar-extreme trail) is implementable in pure MQL5; but 'steep angle (~45deg)', 'race back' and 'large bar' are discretionary and must be proxied (chart-degree angle is scale-dependent -> normalized MA slope).
notes: Discretionary retail price-action method (originator eclayf/Clay); the 10 EMA touch and 35/50 touch are the same mechanic on different MAs -> one row. Trend def: price + 10 EMA above 35/50 (long) or below (short), with 35/50 tight/parallel = strong trend. Entry at the touch, NOT close-of-bar; SL tightened to ~9 pips then trailed, breakeven common; TP ~9-14 pips fixed (1:1 R:R). H1 confluence used as secondary filter; JPY variant (USDJPY) traded 19:30-21:00 GMT+2 Tokyo window. Screenshots only, no statistical evidence; author himself posts several -9 losses. FX-edge doctrine warns generic M15 MA-pullback rarely survives; kept ELIGIBLE per FF-source precedent (Balke), pipeline is the judge.

source_ref: 'The Dance Continues', ForexFactory thread 460041, user '.Hawk.', c.2014 — https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/460041 . Constrainment-trade description at p.19 ('3-4 small bars formed against 10 EMA restraining price ... constrainment trades off 10 EMA are weak, off 35 much stronger').
concept: Enter a trend continuation when a cluster of progressively smaller bars 'constrains' price against a moving average (weak off the 10 EMA, stronger against the 35 SMA) before price resumes the prevailing trend.
eligibility_reason: Concept (small-bar consolidation against a MA -> trend continuation) is mechanizable in principle, but this thread gives no precise trigger, entry level or exit for it beyond the shared ~9-pip stop model; underspecified here.
notes: Second of the method's two approaches ('pick one - constrainment or touch trades'). Far less specified than the touch trade in this thread; full definition lives in the linked 'Dance Manual' / 'Three Main Trades of The Dance' PDFs not present in this file. Shares the same 3-MA trend framework and exit model. Revisit / define precisely only if the touch-trade variant shows edge first.

source_ref: forexalien, 'Alien's Extraterrestrial Visual Systems', ForexFactory thread 463573, 2017, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/463573 (PDF captures opening posts, forum pages 1-3)
concept: Discretionary visual read of RSIOMA (>50 long, <50 short, >80/<20 strong trend) aligned across D1-to-M1, confirmed by a 5-speed ADX and an RSIOMA-vs-delayed-stochastic 'rubber band' separation, to ride momentum trends.
eligibility_reason: Purely discretionary visual method: no numeric entry trigger, no exit/stop/take-profit or sizing rules stated; depends on proprietary custom indicators (RSIOMA variant with extra oscillator, 'D Delay Stochastic.ex4') with unknown internals.
notes: Author self-describes as 'a Visual type of trader'; entries are eyeballed ('look for a place to start thinking about shorting'), exits are 'ride the trend to the end' / discretionary monitoring. Components mentioned but never fully specified: (1) RSIOMA 50/80/20 level system, (2) multi-timeframe wave alignment D1-M1, (3) 5-speed ADX 7/21/42/89/144 all-up/all-down trend gate, (4) RSIOMA+delayed-stochastic separation setup, (5) price-vs-RSIOMA divergence for reversals. Edge claims are marketing ('98% accuracy', hindsight chart examples) with no evidence trail. Would revisit only if OWNER supplies a fully mechanized rule set and the custom indicator sources; as posted it is not implementable.

source_ref: txfxtrader (originator), with indicators by Groenie ('Hitman') and Nonor ('dashboardRSI55'), 'MTF RSI Trading System', ForexFactory thread #504229, 2014. https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/504229
concept: Take the RSI of the last closed bar on each selected timeframe (M1-D1); go long only when RSI is above 50 on ALL of them and short only when below 50 on all, i.e. a multi-timeframe momentum-alignment filter with fixed TP/SL.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic MTF RSI-vs-50 alignment with fixed SL/TP; pure-indicator, no ML, FX data available via .DWX, and FTMO/DXZ-compatible once the optional progressive MM is dropped in favor of RISK_FIXED.
notes: Forum retail system; edge evidence purely anecdotal (~10%/month ROI claim, author admits no backtesting, disclaimer 'what works today may not work tomorrow'). Signal uses the PREVIOUS closed bar per timeframe and the threshold is the 50 line (not classic 30/70). Variants collapse to one core rule set: RSI period 2 or 3 (Groenie 'Hitman' indi) or 55 (Nonor dashboard); TP/SL 30/20 pips or later 60/30; D1 optional, H4 added mid-thread as a noise filter. Pair screen: spread < 4 pips (later <3) and 5-day ADR > 60 pips. Optional flat-close at end of traded session. A progressive/martingale MM (0.1,0.1,0.2,0.4,0.8; cap 5 entries, ~2% cumulative) was proposed then EXPLICITLY REMOVED by the author to avoid confusion, so the core carries per-trade stops and is not a stopless martingale. Trigger timeframe is under-specified (lowest aligned bar); M15 chosen as a representative execution TF for the MTF confluence.

source_ref: StingrayEA, 'Bollinger Band & Stochastic', ForexFactory thread 506226 (27 posts, 2 pages), retail forex forum, ~2019 (thread references Bitcoin trading and 2019-era handles), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/506226-bollinger-band-stochastic
concept: Price crossing a Bollinger band confirmed by a Stochastic main/signal cross and a reversal candlestick triggers a directional entry.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 indicator combo (BB 20/2, Stoch 14/3/3), deterministic, FX H1 .DWX data available, fixed SL 50 / TP 50 / trailing 15 compatible with FTMO/DXZ.
notes: Generic retail BB+Stochastic combo with no equity curve or backtest evidence; multiple skeptics in-thread (dkrock: stochastic wrong tool, price 'walks the band'; billbss: 'I don't see many winners'). OP's stated breakout framing ('price crosses upper band from below = breakout') conflicts with the reversion logic in Magix's posted MQL4 snippet (fade the band tag on a reversal candle, Stoch overbought/oversold) — one core idea, two directional interpretations. 'Bullish/bearish engulfing' is the reversal-candle filter. Yet-another indicator-swing variant, not an uncorrelated driver.

source_ref: TampaDave (reply in StingrayEA's thread), 'Bollinger Band & Stochastic', ForexFactory thread 506226, retail forex forum, ~2019, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/506226-bollinger-band-stochastic
concept: Enter when price sits outside a Bollinger band and Stochastic crosses back from an extreme (below 20 up / above 80 down), scalping a fixed ~10-pip mean-reversion move.
eligibility_reason: Implementable in pure MQL5 once the Stochastic cross is mechanized and a fixed/ATR SL is chosen; FX M15/M30 .DWX data available; but no stop-loss is ever specified.
notes: Discretionary self-report on a live 0.01-lot account (2 weeks), no logs/backtest. Fixed 10-pip target criticized as non-portable across pairs (reteid2222), who suggests ATR-based SL and SMA20 / EMA100-150 exits; 'decisive crossover' is subjective (author warns against entering on a mere touch). Same indicators as Row 1 but clear mean-reversion logic vs Row 1's breakout framing, hence a separate row; author also mentions trading Bitcoin (crypto, out of universe) and M30 as an alternative TF.

source_ref: "1 Hour after daily open", author Nik13, ForexFactory thread #535657 (~2014, not explicitly dated in the excerpt). https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/535657/1-hour-after-daily-open
concept: After the daily (Sydney) open, the close of the first H1 candle relative to the daily-open line signals directional continuation through the low-volatility pre-London session.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 deterministic daily-open/first-hour-close breakout with fixed 10-pip SL/TP on EURUSD/GBPUSD; .DWX data available, has stops, FTMO/DXZ-compatible.
notes: Base rules: buy if first H1 closes above the daily-open line, sell if below; SL=TP=10 pips; EURUSD+GBPUSD opened together with a basket equity-TP (close both when combined +10 pips). Symmetric 1:1 with very tight stops - author concedes SL likely hit >75% and member Mariodrugs reports a 2-week test as 'not worth'; edge doubtful but cheap to test as a session-structure driver. Optional martingale MM overlay (pages 12-13) is OUT OF SCOPE / incompatible with FTMO+DXZ. imran.78p's discretionary variant (DO +-15-20 entry, opposite-side stop, MA100/200 hourly filter, hold for the day) is VAGUE/manual and not carried as a separate row.

source_ref: "1 Hour after daily open", author Nik13, ForexFactory thread #535657 (~2014, not explicitly dated in the excerpt); 'Another similar system' post. https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/535657/1-hour-after-daily-open
concept: Fade the first post-open hour by placing a pending stop at the daily-open line opposite to the first H1 candle's direction, betting price re-crosses the open.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic pending-stop re-cross of the daily-open line on FX majors; implementable in pure MQL5 with stops, but TP not firmly specified by the author.
notes: Nik13's 'another similar system' (page 13): if the first H1 candle is bearish place a BUY-STOP at the daily-open line, if bullish place a SELL-STOP; SL 10, TP ~10 ('but not sure, may be more'). Distinct edge from Row 1 (re-cross/fade vs continuation), hence a separate row. No forward/backtest evidence provided in the thread.

source_ref: Kosomolate, "No long story - RSI system", ForexFactory thread 539300, 2015, https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/539300-no-long-story-rsi-system
concept: Counter-trend mean-reversion buying when a very short RSI(2) drops below 30 and selling when it rises above 70, expecting a snap-back from the overbought/oversold extreme.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 RSI(2) OBOS with explicit stop (SL 25) and TP 10 + break-even mgmt; deterministic, EURUSD M15 data available, FTMO/DXZ-compatible. But OP's headline backtests (1k->68k over 10y) were not reproducible by two forum members.
notes: RSI period 2, upper 70 / lower 30; entry buy RSI<30 / sell RSI>70; SL 25, TP 10, break-even trigger 5 with BE+ 1 (pips, MT4 EA). Symbol unstated by OP but backtested/discussed on EURUSD M15. Reputability: OP posted only equity-curve images (600% claims), MathTrader7 could not replicate with the attached EA and argues a single unfiltered OBOS signal has no structural edge (RSI in trend = 'power zones', not reversal). Classic Connors-style RSI(2) MR variant — yet another OBOS mean-reversion, not an uncorrelated driver; low edge plausibility despite complete rules.

source_ref: abokwaik, 'ATR Break Out' (ABO Trading System), ForexFactory thread 562470 (year n.d.), https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/562470/atr-break-out
concept: Momentum breakout: at each new bar place buy-stop/sell-stop pending orders an ATR-multiple away from price, so a move beyond average bar size triggers a trend-following trade managed with ATR-scaled SL/TP/trailing.
eligibility_reason: Pure-MQL5 ATR straddle breakout with ATR-based SL/TP/trailing stop and risk-% sizing; deterministic; FX-majors + Gold H1 data available via .DWX; each order carries its own stop (not martingale/grid).
notes: One core rule set. Entry: at start of each new bar, place pending stop orders at BreakOut_ATR_mult x ATR(period, default 50) from price (straddle both sides; optional 'Follow Last Bar Direction' filters to one side); untriggered pendings deleted and re-placed each bar until filled. Exits: SL = SL_ATR_mult, TP = TP_ATR_mult, plus ATR trailing stop. Params stated: BO mult 2-3, SL 2-4, TP 4-20, TS 6, ATR period 50; optional MACD+RSI filters (suggested by auvergnat78) to cut false signals. Variants = same core: 'default' (BO x3 + RSI on) vs abokwaik 'crazy'/aggressive (BO x2, filters off, MaxOrders 99, multiple simultaneous orders). Also run on XAUUSD M15, GBP/USD M30, USD/JPY, USD/CAD (instrument-agnostic momentum). Trading-Time window configurable (author uses European session for EURUSD). Caveats: forum-sourced (reputability); user Kubiasty reported persistent losses, author admits Gold-M15 gives many false signals in ranges and default settings 'fire mostly at red-news times' (event-vol angle worth isolating); MaxOrders up to 99 / multi-order stacking = concentration risk, implement with capped max orders. MT4 .ex4 originals only (rebuild in V5 MQL5).

source_ref: Sis.yphus, "A Proven Simple Strategy (2MAs, 1 RSI)", ForexFactory thread 574065, ~2016 (tests Jan 2010-Dec 2015). https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/574065/a-proven-simple-strategy-2mas-1-rsi
concept: Short-term counter-move entry within a trend: when a 2-period RSI hits an extreme and price pulls back to the fast 5 MA while staying on the 200 MA's trend side, fade the pullback and exit when price closes back across the 5 MA.
eligibility_reason: Deterministic MA/RSI mean-reversion on FX majors daily; pure MQL5, no ML, single fixed-lot position, low reported per-pair DD (~6%), FTMO/DXZ-compatible; classic Connors RSI-2 variant.
notes: Long: close>200MA AND close<5MA AND RSI(2)<5. Short: close<200MA AND close>5MA AND RSI(2)>95. Exit at close of candle that touches/crosses 5MA (Option 1) or immediately on touch (Option 2). No hard SL/TP by design (author found none improved it); 5MA exit is the risk cap, framework overlay adds DD guards. Tested EU/GU/AU/NU/UJ/UCHF/UCAD, avg PF 1.31 / avg maxDD 6.19% in-sample (NZDUSD losing at PF 0.95). VARIANTS discussed in thread (same core rules): H1 (dafa: best TF for EU), M15/M5 intraday tests; RSI thresholds 30/70 instead of 5/95 (BillYon/dafa); optional fixed SL + trailing-stop exit (Shemski/abokwaik) gave lower DD on H1. TwoMA_OneRSI.ex4 posted by abokwaik as reference. Reputability: retail forum, in-sample only, modest edge (PF ~1.1-1.3), a swing/short-term-MR FX variant of which the farm likely holds several -> MED not HIGH.

source_ref: swilliam (opener), "TMT Scalping System" (Trend-Momentum-Trendline), ForexFactory thread 577639, 2018 (indicator zip uploaded Jul 22, 2018); https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/577639/tmt-scalping-system . Original system credited by the opener to BabyPips Free Trading Systems (secondary source, not linked).
concept: Trade a trendline break out of an EMA pullback in the direction of the daily-open bias and EMA(7)/EMA(20) trend, confirmed by RSI and MFI momentum filters.
eligibility_reason: Defining entry trigger (hand-drawn trendline from swing + break) and exits are explicitly discretionary per the thread ('no EA draws the trendlines correctly', 'exit depends on trader'); RSI/MFI filter thresholds are unstated custom-indicator colors, so no deterministic rule set exists as described.
notes: Reconstructable-but-new variant: dropping the trendline and using a mechanical close-back-through-EMA(7) trigger inside a daily-open + EMA(7)>EMA(20) trend, with RSI>50 / MFI>50 as momentum gates and fixed SL 10-20p / RR 1:1-1:3, is a generic FX trend-pullback scalper (Sam68 argues the trendline is secondary and the MA-cross + daily-candle + two momentum 'escalators' are the core) — but that is a substituted, under-specified system, not the one stated. Cost drag severe at 10-20 pip targets on FX. Timeframe variants M1/M5/M15 mentioned; some posters (toppo qqq, Hops) applied it to DAX30/GER30 with wider 35-40 pip SL and traded without SL. Forum source, no track-record evidence beyond screenshot claims. Revisit only if a distinct mechanical breakout definition and indicator thresholds are chosen — treat as a new candidate, not this thread's system.
